Explaination: Untrusted request URI data can flow into the HTTP Location header. This is a header-manipulation/open-redirect style sink, not a direct XSS sink, but it is still potentially security-relevant.",
  "response": "{
  "judgement": "FP",
  "analysis": "The reported dataflow is largely real, but it does not produce CWE-079. `DefaultKeycloakContext.getUri()` wraps the request `UriInfo`, and `KeycloakUriInfo.getAbsolutePathBuilder()` reuses `delegate.getAbsolutePath()`, so request-derived URL components can reach the response `Location` header. However, the sink is `HttpHeaders.LOCATION` on a `201 Created` response, not HTML/JavaScript output. That cannot by itself trigger XSS, and this endpoint is not issuing a browser redirect status. So the core error is a wrong sink/CWE match (B1), with a secondary prompt/CWE deviation (C1) because the report itself describes a header-manipulation/open-redirect style issue rather than XSS. This is not an A1/A2 case: the path is reachable enough; the false positive comes from misclassifying the sink, not from an impossible path. It also does not cause a crash. Risk is further reduced because the appended path segment is a server-generated ID and the endpoint requires admin `manage-clients` permission.",
  "FP_Reason": [
    "B1"
  ],
  "evidence": "1) Request-derived source path exists: `uriInfo.put(type, new KeycloakUriInfo(session, type, getHttpRequest().getUri()));` in `services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/services/DefaultKeycloakContext.java:93`. `KeycloakUriInfo` then does `absolutePath = delegate.getAbsolutePath();` and `return UriBuilder.fromUri(getAbsolutePath());` in `server-spi/src/main/java/org/keycloak/models/KeycloakUriInfo.java:83-92`.\n2) The sink is only a response header, not page output: `response.setStatus(Response.Status.CREATED.getStatusCode());` and `response.addHeader(HttpHeaders.LOCATION, session.getContext().getUri().getAbsolutePathBuilder().path(clientInitialAccessModel.getId()).build().toString());` in `services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/services/resources/admin/ClientInitialAccessResource.java:112-113`. `QuarkusHttpResponse.addHeader` just calls `addResponseHeader(name, value)` in `quarkus/runtime/src/main/java/org/keycloak/quarkus/runtime/integration/resteasy/QuarkusHttpResponse.java:54-55`.\n3) The dynamic appended segment is server-generated, not attacker-controlled: `entity.setId(KeycloakModelUtils.generateId());` in `model/jpa/src/main/java/org/keycloak/models/jpa/RealmAdapter.java:2269-2270`.\n4) The endpoint is admin-only: `auth.clients().requireManage();` in `services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/services/resources/admin/ClientInitialAccessResource.java:88`.\n5) Keycloak intentionally supports request/proxy-derived URL generation for `Location` in configured deployments, and tests show untrusted proxy headers are ignored unless enabled/trusted: see `quarkus/tests/integration/src/test/java/org/keycloak/it/cli/dist/ProxyHostnameV2DistTest.java:126-143`.",
  "new_reason:": ""
}"
}

{
  "response": "{
  "judgement": "FP",
  "analysis": "The reported source `JsonNode.asText()` can indeed carry attacker-controlled text, but the sink is not an HTML/JS rendering sink in Keycloak. This code lives in `integration/client-cli/admin-cli`, and the only reachable usage prints CSV output to stdout for a command-line user. CWE-079 requires untrusted data to be emitted into a browser-executed context such as HTML, a template, or DOM-generating output. That does not happen here. The report assumes that the generic `Consumer<String>` parameter could be an HTML renderer, but in the actual codebase no such consumer exists. So this is a false positive, primarily because the sink was misidentified (B1); the report also shows shallow project-context reasoning because it stops at a hypothetical sink instead of the real call site.",
  "FP_Reason": ["B1"],
  "evidence": "`OutputUtil.printAsCsv(object, fields, unquoted)` hardwires the sink to `IoUtil::printOut` at `integration/client-cli/admin-cli/src/main/java/org/keycloak/client/cli/util/OutputUtil.java:53-55`. `IoUtil.printOut` is just `System.out.println(msg)` at `integration/client-cli/admin-cli/src/main/java/org/keycloak/client/cli/util/IoUtil.java:162-164`, so the data is written to terminal/stdout, not to HTML. The only production call site is `printAsCsv(rootNode, returnFields, unquoted)` in `integration/client-cli/admin-cli/src/main/java/org/keycloak/client/admin/cli/commands/AbstractRequestCmd.java:374-379`, inside CLI response printing. The module entrypoint is `KcAdmMain.main(...)` at `integration/client-cli/admin-cli/src/main/java/org/keycloak/client/admin/cli/KcAdmMain.java:54-55`, confirming this is a command-line tool rather than a web endpoint. A repo-wide search finds no alternate invocation of the 4-argument `printAsCsv(..., Consumer<String>)` overload with any HTML-rendering consumer. Even if malicious text reaches `node.asText()` at `OutputUtil.java:103`, it is only printed as CLI text and will not execute as browser script.",
  "new_reason:": ""
}",

Explaination: The source is data read from a JsonNode via asText(), which can reflect end-user input, and it flows through a public method parameterized by a Consumer<String> sink. If that consumer renders the CSV/string into an HTML response without escaping, this can enable XSS or similar injection. No sanitizer is present on the path."

2:
{
  "response": "{
  "judgement": "FP",
  "analysis": "This report is a false positive for CWE-079. The reported sink is not an HTML/JavaScript rendering context; it is a `Location` response header used for a `302` redirect. In `AbstractSamlAuthenticationHandler`, the value returned by `sessionStore.getRedirectUri()` is only passed to `facade.getResponse().setHeader(\"Location\", redirectUri)` and then the response ends. That can at most be relevant to redirect/header issues, not cross-site scripting. The report itself effectively acknowledges this by saying the path is 'more indicative of header injection/response splitting than XSS'. In addition, the specific reported source (`URI.getPath()`) is only used to derive `contextPath` and then passed through `KeycloakUriBuilder.replacePath()`, which calls `Encode.encodePath()`. So the request path is normalized into a relative path prefix, not reflected into executable browser content. The final redirect target is built as `baseUri + \"/\" + redirectTo`, which keeps it as a path-style redirect target rather than a script execution sink.",
  "FP_Reason": [
    "B1"
  ],
  "evidence": "Sink mismatch: `adapters/saml/core/src/main/java/org/keycloak/adapters/saml/profile/AbstractSamlAuthenticationHandler.java:497-500` shows `String redirectUri = sessionStore.getRedirectUri();` followed by `facade.getResponse().setHeader(\"Location\", redirectUri);` and status `302`. This is a redirect header, not HTML output. Source handling: `adapters/saml/wildfly-elytron/src/main/java/org/keycloak/adapters/saml/elytron/ElytronSamlSessionStore.java:192-201` computes `path = uri.getPath();`, `contextPath = path.substring(0, path.indexOf(relativePath));`, then `String baseUri = KeycloakUriBuilder.fromUri(path).replacePath(contextPath).build().toString();`. Sanitization/normalization: `common/src/main/java/org/keycloak/common/util/KeycloakUriBuilder.java:792-798` shows `replacePath()` calls `Encode.encodePath(path)`, and `common/src/main/java/org/keycloak/common/util/Encode.java:252-254` shows `encodePath()` performs path encoding. Redirect construction: `adapters/saml/core/src/main/java/org/keycloak/adapters/saml/SamlUtil.java:69-95` returns either `baseUri` or `baseUri + \"/\" + redirectTo`; this is still only a redirect target. Therefore the reported flow does not create a CWE-079 XSS condition in real-world execution.",
  "new_reason:": ""
}",

1:
{
  "response": "{
  "judgement": "FP",
  "analysis": "The reported source is real and the control-flow path is plausibly reachable after a successful SAML login, especially for the IdP-initiated login branch. However, this is not a CWE-079 issue. The sink chosen by the tool is `setHeader(\"Location\", redirectUri)`, which produces a 302 redirect and ends the response; it does not place attacker-controlled data into HTML, JavaScript, or any browser-executed page context. In other words, IRIS found a redirect/header flow, not an XSS sink. The code also treats `redirectTo` as an application redirect target by prepending a computed `baseUri`, so the feature is intended as a post-login redirect helper rather than page content generation. Therefore this report is a false positive for CWE-079 due to incorrect sink classification.",
  "FP_Reason": [
    "B1"
  ],
  "evidence": "Source: `SamlUtil.getRedirectTo()` reads request-controlled input from `facade.getRequest().getQueryParamValue(\"redirectTo\")` and, alternatively, from `facade.getRequest().getFirstParam(GeneralConstants.RELAY_STATE)` (`adapters/saml/core/src/main/java/org/keycloak/adapters/saml/SamlUtil.java:69-83`). Structure of the value: `buildRedirectTo()` constructs `baseUri + \"/\" + redirectTo` instead of rendering it into HTML (`SamlUtil.java:91-95`). Reachability: `ElytronSamlSessionStore.getRedirectUri()` only calls `SamlUtil.getRedirectTo(...)` when there is no saved original request (`adapters/saml/wildfly-elytron/src/main/java/org/keycloak/adapters/saml/elytron/ElytronSamlSessionStore.java:188-202`); in the normal SP-initiated flow, `AbstractInitiateLogin.challenge()` first saves the original request (`adapters/saml/core/src/main/java/org/keycloak/adapters/saml/AbstractInitiateLogin.java:65-67`). Sink: after authentication, the value is only used as `facade.getResponse().setHeader(\"Location\", redirectUri); facade.getResponse().setStatus(302); facade.getResponse().end();` (`adapters/saml/core/src/main/java/org/keycloak/adapters/saml/profile/AbstractSamlAuthenticationHandler.java:497-501`). In the Elytron facade, `setHeader` delegates to `response.addResponseHeader(name, value)` (`adapters/saml/wildfly-elytron/src/main/java/org/keycloak/adapters/saml/elytron/ElytronHttpFacade.java:353-359`). There is no HTML response body or script/DOM sink in this path. Real use scenario: the adapter test data configures IdP-initiated SSO relay state as `redirectTo=/foo`, showing this parameter is intended as a post-login path redirect, not page content injection (`testsuite/integration-arquillian/tests/base/src/test/resources/adapter-test/keycloak-saml/testsaml.json:209-210`).",
  "new_reason:": "This path could merit separate review for non-XSS issues such as redirect semantics, but the report as filed does not establish CWE-079."
}",

  "report": "Vulnerability Path:

Explaination: User-controlled Origin header is copied into an HTTP response header. This is not a Cross-Site Scripting sink, but it can still be a relevant header-injection/CORS issue if untrusted input is reflected without validation.",
  "response": "{
  "judgement": "FP",
  "analysis": "Step 1: the source is real because `Origin` is requester-controlled, but the selected sink is wrong for CWE-079. The reported sink is `response.setHeader(\"Access-Control-Allow-Origin\", origin)`, which only writes an HTTP response header and does not place attacker data into HTML, JavaScript, or a browser-executable DOM context. Therefore this path does not satisfy the sink semantics of Cross-Site Scripting. Step 2: the dataflow itself is syntactically reachable, but for normal non-preflight requests Keycloak returns early unless the origin is in `allowedOrigins` or `*` is configured, so ordinary CORS responses are guarded by configured policy. Preflight paths can reflect the origin more broadly, but they are still only CORS header handlers. Step 3: A1/A2/D* are not the decisive cause here; the main error is the sink/CWE mismatch. Step 4: the report explanation itself admits this is 'not a Cross-Site Scripting sink', which is consistent with a false positive for CWE-079.",
  "FP_Reason": [
    "B1"
  ],
  "evidence": "In `services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/services/cors/DefaultCors.java` lines 136-150, Keycloak reads `String origin = request.getHttpHeaders().getRequestHeaders().getFirst(ORIGIN_HEADER);` and then writes `response.setHeader(ACCESS_CONTROL_ALLOW_ORIGIN, origin);`. In `quarkus/runtime/src/main/java/org/keycloak/quarkus/runtime/integration/resteasy/QuarkusHttpResponse.java` lines 59-60, `setHeader` is implemented as `requestContext.serverResponse().setResponseHeader(name, value);`. That is response-header metadata, not an HTML/JS rendering sink. Also, `DefaultCors.java` lines 142-147 show a guard for non-preflight flows: if `allowedOrigins` is null or does not contain the request origin (and no wildcard is configured), the method returns without reflecting it. `services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/protocol/oidc/utils/WebOriginsUtils.java` lines 34-44 show these allowed origins come from configured client web origins / resolved redirect URIs. `services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/services/resources/account/CorsPreflightService.java` lines 19-23 shows reachable `preflight()` uses are `@OPTIONS` CORS handlers. So the concrete path is a CORS-header reflection path, not a browser script execution path; it does not establish CWE-079 and does not show a crash vector.",

}"

Explaination: User-controllable attributes from a public/provider-facing mapper are passed directly into FreeMarker template rendering; if the template outputs them in HTML, this can lead to XSS without sanitization.",
  "response": "{
  "judgement": "FP",
  "analysis": "Primary FP type is B1. The reported source is misidentified: `attributeMapper` is not attacker-controlled input, but an internal server-side SPI callback exposed through `LoginFormsProvider.setAttributeMapper(...)`. In the real codebase, the production uses of this mapper only rewrite existing model beans for organization/login behavior; they do not let an external user supply arbitrary HTML-bearing attributes. The sink is also overstated: passing a map into FreeMarker is not itself XSS in Keycloak, because `.ftl` templates are rendered with `HTMLOutputFormat`, and places that intentionally allow HTML use `kcSanitize(...)?no_esc`. So the report shows a generic map-to-template path, but not a concrete user-input-to-unsafe-HTML path that is exploitable in normal Keycloak deployments. Secondary FP aspects are shallow project-level reasoning (A1) and missed escaping/sanitization context (D1).",
  "FP_Reason": [
    "B1"
  ],
  "evidence": "`FreeMarkerLoginFormsProvider.processTemplate` applies the mapper and forwards the result to FreeMarker at `services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/forms/login/freemarker/FreeMarkerLoginFormsProvider.java:604-611`. However, the mapper API itself is only an internal Java SPI method declared at `server-spi-private/src/main/java/org/keycloak/forms/login/LoginFormsProvider.java:170`. The real production call sites are internal only, in `services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/organization/authentication/authenticators/browser/OrganizationAuthenticator.java:252-259`, `341-360`, `405-413`, and `435-443`; these lambdas only wrap existing beans such as `AuthenticationContextBean`, `IdentityProviderBean`, and `RealmBean`, or set `USERNAME_HIDDEN`, rather than injecting raw request data into the template model. On the rendering side, `services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/theme/freemarker/DefaultFreeMarkerProvider.java:56-63` sets `cfg.setOutputFormat(HTMLOutputFormat.INSTANCE)` for `.ftl` templates, and `:28-31` injects `kcSanitize` into the model. Templates explicitly sanitize raw-HTML outputs, for example `themes/src/main/resources/theme/base/login/template.ftl:171` and `themes/src/main/resources/theme/base/login/error.ftl:7`. Other outputs such as `themes/src/main/resources/theme/base/login/info.ftl:7,11`, `themes/src/main/resources/theme/base/login/login.ftl:15`, and `themes/src/main/resources/theme/base/login/template.ftl:134` use normal `${...}` rendering under HTML output format, which is intended to be escaped rather than emitted as raw HTML. Therefore the report does not demonstrate a real external attacker-controlled XSS path in Keycloak core.",
